**[PART I --- RENEWAL TERM SHEET FOR LICENSE A (CRISPR-Ag Suite)]{.underline}**

**1. Parties and Reference**

> • **Licensor:** NovaTrait Genomics Corporation
>
> • **Licensee:** Greenfield Biosciences, Inc.
>
> • **Existing Agreement:** Intellectual Property License Agreement (CRISPR-Ag Suite Gene-Editing Platform), License No. NT-GF-2015-001, dated July 1, 2015
>
> • **Existing Initial Term Expiration:** June 30, 2025
>
> • **Renewal Notice Deadline (per Section 14.2):** March 31, 2025

**2. Renewal Term**

> • **Proposed Renewal Term:** Five (5) years, commencing July 1, 2025 and expiring June 30, 2030.
>
> • **Further Renewal Option:** One (1) additional five-year renewal, subject to mutual written agreement and a renewed independent fair market value (\"IFMV\") assessment delivered not later than ninety (90) days prior to expiration.

**3. Royalty Rate**

> • **Current Rate (Section 7.1):** Four and one-half percent (4.5%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products.
>
> • **Proposed Rate:** Six and one-quarter percent (6.25%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products.
>
> • **Cap Compliance:** The proposed rate represents approximately one hundred thirty-eight and nine-tenths percent (138.9%) of the current Royalty Rate and is therefore within the one hundred fifty percent (150%) cap set forth in Section 14.3(a), such that no IFMV assessment is required as a condition to the increase.
>
> • **Rationale:** Current market comparables for gene-editing platform licenses of comparable scope and duration range from five and one-half percent (5.5%) to seven percent (7.0%), based on NovaTrait\'s review of publicly available agreements and the 2024 Evergreen IP Royalty Benchmarking Report (Q2 2024 release). NovaTrait\'s proposed rate is positioned near the midpoint of this range.

**4. Minimum Annual Royalty**

> • **Current (Section 7.2):** Four Million United States Dollars (\$4,000,000) per calendar year.
>
> • **Proposed:** Eight Million United States Dollars (\$8,000,000) per calendar year.
>
> • **Step-Up Mechanic:** For the Renewal Term, the minimum annual royalty shall be paid on a quarterly basis of \$2,000,000 per quarter, creditable against running royalties accrued in such quarter. Any quarterly shortfall shall be payable within forty-five (45) days following the end of the applicable calendar quarter.

**5. Net Sales Definition (Revised)**

Section 1.14 (Net Sales) shall be amended and restated in its entirety to read substantially as follows:

\> \"Net Sales\" means the gross amount invoiced by Greenfield, its Affiliates, and its Sublicensees for sales, leases, or other dispositions of Licensed Products to Third Parties in arm\'s-length transactions, less only: (a) customary and reasonable trade, cash, and quantity discounts actually granted and taken; and (b) sales, value-added, excise, and similar taxes directly imposed on such sales to the extent separately invoiced and remitted to the relevant governmental authority.

The following deductions are eliminated from the existing definition: (i) freight, shipping, handling, and insurance costs; (ii) credits and allowances for returns, rejections, recalls, and chargebacks; and (iii) bad debt write-offs. NovaTrait submits that the revised definition is consistent with current industry practice and with the definition used in NovaTrait\'s most recent comparable agreements.

**6. Field of Use (Revised)**

Section 1.7 (Field of Use) shall be amended and restated as follows:

\> \"Field of Use\" means the genetic modification of corn (Zea mays), soybean (Glycine max), and wheat (Triticum aestivum, Triticum durum, and other commercially relevant Triticum species) for the development of herbicide tolerance traits, including the engineering of crop varieties expressing one or more transgenic or cisgenic constructs conferring herbicide tolerance to glyphosate, glufosinate, dicamba, 2,4-D, HPPD inhibitors, and ACCase inhibitors.

**Notable Changes:**

> • **Added:** Wheat (Triticum spp.) as a new licensed crop species.
>
> • **Removed:** Insect resistance traits (including constructs expressing Bt-derived or non-Bt insecticidal proteins) are removed from the Field of Use.

**Rationale for Removal of Insect Resistance:** NovaTrait has entered into an exclusive licensing arrangement with another agricultural biotechnology company for the insect resistance application of the CRISPR-Ag Suite platform in field crops, and cannot continue to grant non-exclusive rights in that field. NovaTrait is prepared to discuss a transition mechanism for Greenfield\'s existing commercial insect resistance product lines, if any, on mutually acceptable terms (which may include a separate, non-exclusive, transitional license with a finite sunset period).

**7. Territory.** No change. Section 1.18 shall remain worldwide, excluding the People\'s Republic of China (including Hong Kong and Macau) and the Republic of India.

**8. Sublicense Pass-Through (Revised)**

> • **Current (Section 10.3):** Thirty-five percent (35%) of Sublicensee Royalties pass through to NovaTrait.
>
> • **Proposed:** Fifty percent (50%) of Sublicensee Royalties shall pass through to NovaTrait, subject to Greenfield\'s continued obligation to collect and remit such amounts within sixty (60) days following receipt. \"Sublicensee Royalties\" shall continue to be calculated on a Net Sales basis at the sublicensee level.
>
> • **Application to Existing Sublicensees:** The revised pass-through rate shall apply to Sublicensee Royalties accrued from and after the effective date of the Renewal Agreement. It shall not apply retroactively to royalties already received by Greenfield from the Prairielands Seed Cooperative or any other Sublicensee on or before such effective date.

**9. Improvements (Revised)**

Section 8.1 (Ownership of Improvements) shall be amended and restated as follows:

\> All Improvements to the Licensed Technology made by or on behalf of either Party during the Renewal Term shall be jointly owned by NovaTrait and Greenfield, without any obligation to account to the other. Greenfield hereby grants and agrees to grant to NovaTrait an irrevocable, perpetual, worldwide, fully paid-up, royalty-free, sublicensable, and transferable license to practice and exploit any such Improvements for any and all purposes, whether within or outside the Field of Use. NovaTrait hereby grants and agrees to grant to Greenfield, for the duration of the Renewal Term only, a non-exclusive, non-sublicensable, royalty-bearing license to practice any such Improvements within the Field of Use.

**Comparison to Current Terms:** The current Section 8.1 provides that Greenfield is the sole owner of all Improvements conceived or reduced to practice by Greenfield, subject to a non-exclusive license back to NovaTrait at a commercially reasonable royalty to be mutually agreed. NovaTrait views the shift to joint ownership as a more equitable reflection of the Parties\' collaborative investment in the platform during the Renewal Term.

**10. Anti-Stacking / Third-Party Royalty Offset (New)**

NovaTrait proposes the addition of a new Section 7.7 as follows:

\> If Greenfield is required, as a condition of freedom-to-operate with respect to any Licensed Product, to pay royalties to one or more unaffiliated Third Parties under patents that are necessary to make, use, or sell such Licensed Product (including, without limitation, patents identified in a freedom-to-operate opinion rendered by qualified outside IP counsel), Greenfield shall be entitled to credit against the running royalties otherwise payable to NovaTrait under Section 7.1 with respect to such Licensed Product an amount equal to fifty percent (50%) of the royalties actually paid by Greenfield to such Third Parties; provided, however, that (i) the aggregate credit against royalties payable to NovaTrait in any calendar quarter shall not exceed twenty-five percent (25%) of the royalties otherwise payable; and (ii) in no event shall the effective royalty rate payable to NovaTrait on Net Sales of any Licensed Product fall below four and three-quarters percent (4.75%).

**11. Performance Requirements**

> • Continued application of the diligence obligations set forth in existing Section 4.1, with updated milestones to reflect the expanded wheat Field of Use (deliverables: (a) initiation of at least one (1) field trial in wheat in North America within thirty-six (36) months of the Renewal Effective Date; (b) regulatory submission for at least one (1) wheat Licensed Product in the United States or Canada within sixty (60) months of the Renewal Effective Date).
>
> • Failure to meet the diligence milestones triggers the 180-day cure period and conversion-to-non-exclusive remedy already set forth in existing Section 4.3 (unchanged).

**12. Audit Rights**

No change to frequency or notice period. Audit rights remain once per calendar year under existing Section 9.2.

**13. Most-Favored-Licensee.** NovaTrait proposes to maintain existing Section 14.5 (MFL) with no changes, including the carve-outs for (a) licenses granted to academic or non-profit research institutions and (b) licenses entered into in connection with the settlement of litigation.

**14. Schedule of Licensed Patents (as amended)**

The Licensed Patents schedule shall be updated to include all post-2015 continuation, continuation-in-part, divisional, and foreign counterpart filings, as enumerated in Appendix A to this Proposal. The material foundational patents remain:

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Patent No.**                          **Filed**       **Issued**      **Expiration**   **Contribution to Portfolio Value**
  --------------------------------------- --------------- --------------- ---------------- -------------------------------------
  U.S. 9,234,117                          Jun. 14, 2013   Jan. 12, 2016   Mar. 12, 2028    Foundational

  U.S. 9,234,118                          Sep. 22, 2013   Jan. 12, 2016   Dec. 19, 2028    Foundational

  U.S. 9,651,002                          Mar. 8, 2014    May 16, 2017    Mar. 8, 2029     Supporting

  U.S. 10,112,445                         Nov. 3, 2016    Oct. 30, 2018   Nov. 3, 2031     Supporting

  Foreign counterparts (EP, AR, BR, AU)   Various         Various         Various          Supporting
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

NovaTrait acknowledges that U.S. Patent No. 9,234,117 is scheduled to expire on March 12, 2028, in the middle of the proposed Renewal Term. NovaTrait submits, however, that the foundational methods disclosed in that patent continue to be covered by later-issued continuation patents (including 10,112,445) and by substantial unpatented know-how transferred under existing Article 3, such that the post-2028 commercial value of the License A technology stack is not materially impaired by the expiration of 9,234,117 alone.

**15. Governing Law**

No change. The Renewal Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the Commonwealth of Massachusetts.

\-\--

**[PART II --- RENEWAL TERM SHEET FOR LICENSE B (TraitForge)]{.underline}**

**1. Parties and Reference**

> • **Licensor:** NovaTrait Genomics Corporation
>
> • **Licensee:** Greenfield Biosciences, Inc.
>
> • **Existing Agreement:** Plant Trait Expression Platform License Agreement (TraitForge), dated January 1, 2018
>

>

NovaTrait is prepared, by way of a short-form standstill memorandum, to confirm that License B shall remain in full force and effect pursuant to Section 12.6 (Standstill Provision) for an initial period of up to one hundred eighty (180) days, extendable by mutual agreement, pending completion of the Renewal Agreement. Greenfield should not construe any provision of this Proposal as NovaTrait\'s waiver of any contractual right, including any right under Section 12.2, and NovaTrait\'s rights are expressly reserved.

**3. Renewal Term**

>
> • **Further Renewal Option:** One (1) additional renewal term of three (3) years, subject to mutual written agreement.

**4. Royalty Rate**

> • **Current (Section 4.1):** Three and three-quarters percent (3.75%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products.
>
> • **Proposed:** Five percent (5.0%) of Net Sales of Licensed Products.
>

**5. Minimum Annual Royalty**

> • **Current (Section 4.2):** Two Million Five Hundred Thousand United States Dollars (\$2,500,000) per calendar year.
>
> • **Proposed:** Five Million United States Dollars (\$5,000,000) per calendar year, payable in quarterly installments of \$1,250,000, creditable against running royalties accrued in such quarter.

**6. Field of Use**

No change. The Field of Use remains the expression of drought tolerance and nutrient uptake efficiency traits in corn (Zea mays), soybean (Glycine max), and cotton (Gossypium spp.).

**7. Territory.** No change. Territory remains worldwide, subject to sublicensee-specific geographic scopes (including the Argentina/Brazil scope of the Prairielands sublicense).

**8. Audit Rights (Revised)**

Section 5.2 (Audit Rights) shall be amended and restated as follows:

\> NovaTrait shall have the right, not more than twice (2 times) per calendar year, upon not less than fifteen (15) business days\' prior written notice to Greenfield, to cause an independent, nationally recognized certified public accounting firm reasonably acceptable to Greenfield to inspect and audit the books and records of Greenfield relevant to the calculation and payment of royalties under this Agreement. The scope of each such audit may cover the five (5) preceding calendar years. If any such audit identifies an underpayment by Greenfield of more than five percent (5%) for any audited period, Greenfield shall bear the reasonable out-of-pocket costs of the audit, in addition to paying the underpayment plus interest at the rate set forth in Section 5.4.

**Comparison to Current Terms:** Current Section 5.2 permits audit not more than once per calendar year upon thirty (30) calendar days\' prior notice.

**9. Return of Biological Materials (Revised)**

Section 7.4 (Effects of Termination / Return of Materials) shall be amended and restated as follows:

\> Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason (other than termination by Greenfield for material breach by NovaTrait), Greenfield shall, within thirty (30) calendar days, return to NovaTrait or destroy (at NovaTrait\'s written election) all proprietary expression vectors, plasmids, transformation cassettes, seed stocks, cell lines, and other Biological Materials in Greenfield\'s possession or control, and shall certify such return or destruction in a written certification signed by an officer of Greenfield.

**Comparison to Current Terms:** Current Section 7.4 provides a ninety (90)-day wind-down period during which Greenfield may continue to use Biological Materials in already-initiated breeding programs and field trials, subject to ongoing royalty obligations, prior to the return or destruction requirement.

**10. Improvements.** Section 8 (Improvements) shall be amended to conform to the joint ownership and royalty-free license-back structure proposed for License A Section 8.1 (see Part I, Item 9 above).

**11. Licensed Patents Schedule**

No changes to the patent schedule other than to reflect additional foreign counterpart filings since 2018. The Licensed Patents continue to include U.S. Patent Nos. 10,443,076; 10,443,077; 10,889,201; and corresponding foreign counterparts.

**12. Standstill / Transition Provisions**

The Renewal Agreement shall retain the Standstill Provision (Section 12.6) with no changes, such that if, at the expiration of the Renewal Term, the Parties are engaged in good-faith negotiations for a successor agreement, the terms of the Renewal Agreement shall continue in force for up to one hundred eighty (180) days.

**13. Governing Law**

No change. Massachusetts law.

\-\--

**[PART III --- RENEWAL TERM SHEET FOR LICENSE C (GenoMap Pro)]{.underline}**

**1. Parties and Reference**

> • **Licensor:** NovaTrait Genomics Corporation
>
> • **Licensee:** Greenfield Biosciences, Inc.
>
> • **Existing Agreement:** GenoMap Pro Software License Agreement, dated March 1, 2020
>
> • **Existing Initial Term Expiration:** February 28, 2025
>
> • **Renewal Notice Deadline (per Section 3.2):** November 30, 2024

**2. Renewal Term**

> • **Proposed Renewal Term:** Three (3) years, commencing March 1, 2025 and expiring February 28, 2028.
>
> • **Rationale for Shorter Term:** Given the pace of development in the agricultural bioinformatics industry and the anticipated release of GenoMap Pro Version 6.x in late 2027, NovaTrait believes a three-year renewal term, with a further renewal option, is appropriate. License C has historically been negotiated on a shorter renewal cycle than the platform licenses (License A and License B), reflecting the nature of software licensing.

**3. License Fee Structure**

> • **Current (Section 4.1, approximate):** \$3,140,000 annual license fee, inclusive of Minor Updates and standard support.
>
> • **Proposed:**
>
> • **Base Annual License Fee:** \$4,800,000
>
> • **Support and Maintenance Fee:** \$305,000 per year (unchanged in structure; rate escalated to reflect expanded support scope)
>
> • **Version 5.x Platform Upgrade Fee:** \$175,000 one-time file conversion and migration fee, payable upon execution of the Renewal Agreement
>
> • **Total Estimated Annual Cost:** Approximately \$5,280,000 (including amortization of the Version 5.x upgrade fee over the three-year term at approximately \$58,000 per year)

**4. Software Version and Upgrade Path**

Upon commencement of the Renewal Term, Greenfield shall transition from GenoMap Pro Version 4.x to GenoMap Pro Version 5.x. Version 5.x includes:

> • Machine-learning-driven trait prediction modules (with published accuracy improvements of approximately 22% over Version 4.x models)
>
> • Updated database schemas optimized for multi-omic inputs
>
> • Native cloud deployment options (AWS, Azure, GCP) with enhanced security controls
>
> • Enterprise-grade single-sign-on and role-based access controls

NovaTrait shall provide reasonable migration assistance (including database schema conversion scripts and up to eighty (80) hours of technical support personnel time) during the three (3) months following the Renewal Effective Date.

**5. Source Code Access (Revised)**

Current Section 5.2 provides Greenfield with the right to access the Source Code of the Software for the limited purpose of internal modification, subject to confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations.

**Proposed:** Section 5.2 shall be deleted in its entirety. Under the Renewal Agreement, Greenfield shall receive Object Code only, consistent with the standard licensing model for GenoMap Pro Version 5.x and in line with NovaTrait\'s enterprise software distribution practices. NovaTrait shall continue to maintain a Source Code Escrow arrangement pursuant to existing Section 5.3, to be released to Greenfield only upon the occurrence of specified Release Events (including NovaTrait insolvency or cessation of support for Version 5.x).

**Existing Modifications:** Any modifications to Version 4.x Source Code developed by Greenfield pursuant to current Section 5.2 shall be deemed Derivative Works and shall be (a) owned jointly by the Parties or (b) at Greenfield\'s election, purchased by NovaTrait for fair market value to be determined by mutual agreement or, failing agreement, by a qualified independent valuation firm.

**6. Data Ownership (Revised)**

Current Section 9.2 provides that Input Data uploaded to the Software by Greenfield remains the sole property of Greenfield. Existing Section 9 does not expressly address ownership of Derivative Data (i.e., outputs, analyses, visualizations, and results generated by the Software based on Input Data).

**Proposed:** A new Section 9.3 shall be added as follows:

\> All Derivative Data generated by the Software through the processing, analysis, transformation, or manipulation of Input Data or other data sources shall be the sole and exclusive property of NovaTrait; provided, however, that NovaTrait hereby grants to Greenfield, during the Renewal Term and subject to Greenfield\'s compliance with the Accepted Use Policy, a non-exclusive, non-transferable, royalty-free license to access, view, export, and use such Derivative Data solely for Greenfield\'s internal research, development, and commercialization purposes within the Field of Use of Licenses A and B. For the avoidance of doubt, Greenfield\'s license to Derivative Data does not permit Greenfield to (i) sublicense such Derivative Data to any Third Party (other than employees and contractors bound by written confidentiality obligations at least as protective as this Agreement), (ii) publish such Derivative Data in any form identifiable to NovaTrait or the Software, or (iii) use such Derivative Data after the expiration or termination of the Renewal Term.

**7. Renewal Options**

A single three-year renewal option, exercisable upon mutual agreement, with fees and license terms to be renegotiated at the time.

**8. Governing Law**

No change. Massachusetts law.

\-\--

**[PART IV --- CROSS-LICENSE PROVISIONS]{.underline}**

**1. Cross-License Term Alignment**

NovaTrait acknowledges that the proposed renewal terms set forth above result in three renewal periods of differing durations:

> • License A: July 1, 2025 -- June 30, 2030 (5 years)
>
> • License B: January 1, 2025 -- December 31, 2029 (5 years)
>
> • License C: March 1, 2025 -- February 28, 2028 (3 years)

NovaTrait is willing to discuss optional term harmonization, including the possibility of a coordinated five (5)-year term for License C at an incremental license fee to be agreed, or a shorter initial term for Licenses A and B. NovaTrait notes, however, that the proposed three-year License C term is appropriate given the pace of GenoMap Pro product evolution.

**2. Coordinated Payment Schedule**

For administrative efficiency, NovaTrait proposes that all running royalties, minimum annual royalties, and License C fees be consolidated into a single quarterly payment statement, with detailed supporting schedules for each Licensed Product and each license.

**3. Most-Favored-Licensee Clauses**

Each Renewal Agreement shall retain its respective existing MFL provisions, provided that the MFL shall be calculated on an agreement-by-agreement basis (rather than across all three agreements).

\-\--

NovaTrait notes that Greenfield\'s actual aggregate cost will depend on (a) future Net Sales growth, (b) utilization of the anti-stacking royalty offset, and (c) the ultimate terms negotiated by the Parties.

**[PART VII --- PROCEDURAL AND TIMING MATTERS]{.underline}**

**1. Existing License B Status (Standstill)**

Because License B\'s Initial Term expired on December 31, 2024 and the 90-day renewal notice window under Section 12.2 has passed, NovaTrait proposes that the Parties promptly execute a written standstill confirmation (consistent with Section 12.6) to document the continued good-faith negotiation and the continued effectiveness of License B pending execution of the Renewal Agreement. A proposed form of standstill confirmation is attached as **Appendix B** to this Proposal.

**2. License C Renewal Notice**

NovaTrait will treat this Proposal, and Greenfield\'s receipt and acknowledgement of this Proposal, as constituting initiation of renewal negotiations for License C under Section 3.2. NovaTrait requests that Greenfield confirm its desire to renew License C in writing on or before November 30, 2024, in order to preserve Greenfield\'s renewal rights.

**3. Prairielands Sublicense Considerations**

NovaTrait is aware that Greenfield\'s sublicense to Prairielands Seed Cooperative, dated May 1, 2021, was drafted to be co-terminus with the Master License (License B). Accordingly, Greenfield should consider the implications of the License B renewal for the Prairielands sublicense, including whether any amendment or renewal of the Prairielands sublicense is required in parallel. NovaTrait is willing to provide its consent to any such amendment, subject to Section 12.4 of the existing Master License, on reasonable terms.

**4. Confidentiality.** This Proposal is confidential and is subject to the confidentiality provisions of each of the Existing Agreements. This Proposal is also a settlement communication within the meaning of Rule 408 of the Federal Rules of Evidence and Rule 408 of the Massachusetts Rules of Evidence and shall not be admissible in any proceeding between the Parties, except as provided by applicable law.

**5. Reservation of Rights**

Nothing in this Proposal constitutes a waiver of any right or remedy of NovaTrait under any of the Existing Agreements or under applicable law. All rights are expressly reserved.

\-\--

1\. **Greenfield Response.** NovaTrait requests Greenfield\'s written response to this Proposal not later than **November 15, 2024**.

2\. **Standstill Confirmation (License B).** Execution of short-form standstill confirmation on or before **December 15, 2024**.

3\. **License C Renewal Notice.** Greenfield\'s written notice of intent to renew License C on or before **November 30, 2024**.

4\. **Working Session.** Proposed week of October 28, 2024 (Cambridge) or week of November 4, 2024 (Raleigh).

5\. **Target Execution Date for Amended and Restated Renewal Agreements:** on or before **March 15, 2025**.

**SUBLICENSE AGREEMENT**

**TraitForge Plant Trait Expression Platform**

**Soybean Drought Tolerance --- Argentina and Brazil**

This Sublicense Agreement (this \"**Agreement**\") is entered into as of May 1, 2021 (the \"**Effective Date**\"), by and between:

**Greenfield Biosciences, Inc.**, a Delaware corporation with its principal offices at 4200 Research Park Drive, Suite 310, Raleigh, NC 27609 (\"**Greenfield**\" or \"**Sublicensor**\");

and

**Prairielands Seed Cooperative**, an Iowa cooperative with its principal offices at 1120 Harvest Road, Des Moines, IA 50309 (\"**Prairielands**\" or \"**Sublicensee**\").

Greenfield and Prairielands are each referred to herein as a \"**Party**\" and collectively as the \"**Parties**.\"

**RECITALS**

**WHEREAS**, NovaTrait Genomics Corporation, a Massachusetts corporation (\"**NovaTrait**\"), and Greenfield are parties to that certain Plant Trait Expression Platform License Agreement dated as of January 1, 2018 (the \"**Master License**\"), pursuant to which NovaTrait granted Greenfield a license to certain proprietary plant trait expression technologies, expression vectors, biological materials, and related intellectual property marketed under the name \"TraitForge\" (the \"**Licensed Technology**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Master License covers U.S. Patent Nos. 10,443,076; 10,443,077; and 10,889,201, together with corresponding foreign filings in the European Patent Office and Brazil, as well as proprietary expression vectors and biological materials (collectively, the \"**Licensed IP**\");

**WHEREAS**, the Master License permits Greenfield to grant sublicenses under the Licensed IP with NovaTrait\'s prior written consent;

**WHEREAS**, Prairielands desires to obtain a sublicense under the Licensed IP for the limited purpose of developing and commercializing soybean varieties incorporating drought tolerance traits using the TraitForge platform for sale in Argentina and Brazil;

**WHEREAS**, NovaTrait has granted its written consent to this sublicense arrangement pursuant to a letter dated April 15, 2021, a copy of which is attached hereto as **Exhibit A**; and

**WHEREAS**, Greenfield desires to grant, and Prairielands desires to accept, such sublicense on the terms and conditions set forth herein.

**NOW, THEREFORE**, in consideration of the mutual covenants and agreements contained herein and for other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which are hereby acknowledged, the Parties agree as follows:

**[Article 1 --- Definitions]{.underline}**

**1.1** \"**Confidential Information**\" means all non-public information disclosed by one Party to the other in connection with this Agreement, including trade secrets, technical data, business plans, financial information, and the terms of this Agreement.

**1.2** \"**Licensed IP**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

**1.3** \"**Licensed Technology**\" has the meaning set forth in the Recitals.

**1.4** \"**Master License**\" means the Plant Trait Expression Platform License Agreement between NovaTrait and Greenfield dated January 1, 2018, as may be amended, renewed, or extended from time to time.

**1.5** \"**Net Sales**\" means the gross amount invoiced by Prairielands or its affiliates for sales of Sublicensed Products to third parties in the Territory, less the following deductions to the extent actually incurred and separately stated on the invoice or otherwise documented:

> \(a\) trade discounts, quantity discounts, and rebates actually allowed and taken;
>
> \(b\) credits or allowances given for rejection, return, or recall of Sublicensed Products;
>
> \(c\) freight, shipping, insurance, and handling charges;
>
> \(d\) sales taxes, value-added taxes, duties, and other governmental charges imposed on the sale; and
>
> \(e\) amounts written off as uncollectible, provided that any subsequent recovery shall be added back to Net Sales.

**1.6** \"**NovaTrait**\" means NovaTrait Genomics Corporation, a Massachusetts corporation with its principal offices at 77 Kendall Square, Building 6, Cambridge, MA 02142.

**1.7** \"**Sublicensed Products**\" means soybean (Glycine max) seed varieties that incorporate drought tolerance traits developed or expressed using the Licensed Technology.

**1.8** \"**Territory**\" means Argentina and Brazil, and no other country or region.

**[Article 2 --- Grant of Sublicense]{.underline}**

**2.1** *Sublicense Grant.* Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement and the Master License, Greenfield hereby grants to Prairielands a non-exclusive, non-transferable sublicense under the Licensed IP to use the Licensed Technology solely for the purpose of developing, producing, and commercializing Sublicensed Products within the Territory during the Term.

**2.2** *Scope Limitations.* The sublicense granted under Section 2.1 is limited to:

> \(a\) **Crop Species:** Soybean (Glycine max) only. Prairielands shall not use the Licensed Technology in connection with any other crop species, including corn, cotton, wheat, or any other plant.
>
> \(b\) **Trait Application:** Drought tolerance traits only. Prairielands shall not use the Licensed Technology for the expression or development of any other trait, including but not limited to nutrient uptake efficiency, herbicide tolerance, or insect resistance.
>
> \(c\) **Geography:** Sales and distribution of Sublicensed Products are limited to Argentina and Brazil. Prairielands shall not sell, distribute, export, or otherwise make Sublicensed Products available outside the Territory.

**2.3** *No Further Sublicensing.* Prairielands shall not sublicense, assign, or otherwise transfer any rights granted under this Agreement to any third party without the prior written consent of both Greenfield and NovaTrait.

**2.4** *Master License Supremacy.* The rights granted to Prairielands under this Agreement are derivative of and subject to the Master License. In the event of any conflict between this Agreement and the Master License, the terms of the Master License shall control to the extent of such conflict. Prairielands acknowledges that it has been afforded the opportunity to review the relevant provisions of the Master License pertaining to field of use, territorial restrictions, and sublicensing.

**2.5** *Compliance with Master License.* Prairielands shall comply with all obligations under the Master License that are applicable to sublicensees, as identified and communicated by Greenfield from time to time. Greenfield shall promptly notify Prairielands of any amendments to the Master License that materially affect Prairielands\' rights or obligations hereunder.

**[Article 3 --- Royalties and Payments]{.underline}**

**3.1** *Royalty Rate.* In consideration of the sublicense granted hereunder, Prairielands shall pay to Greenfield a royalty equal to **two percent (2.0%)** of Net Sales of Sublicensed Products during each calendar quarter of the Term.

**3.2** *Payment Schedule.* Royalty payments shall be due within forty-five (45) days after the end of each calendar quarter (i.e., by February 14, May 15, August 14, and November 14 of each year). Each payment shall be accompanied by a written report setting forth in reasonable detail:

> \(a\) the total quantity of Sublicensed Products sold in each country within the Territory during the quarter;
>
> \(b\) the gross invoiced amounts for such sales;
>
> \(c\) an itemized listing of all permissible deductions under Section 1.5; and
>
> \(d\) the resulting Net Sales and royalty calculation.

**3.3** *Currency.* All royalty payments shall be made in United States Dollars. Where Sublicensed Products are sold in a currency other than U.S. Dollars, the applicable conversion rate shall be the closing mid-market exchange rate published by the Wall Street Journal (or a comparable source mutually agreed upon by the Parties) on the last business day of the applicable calendar quarter.

**3.4** *Late Payments.* Any amount not paid when due shall bear interest from the date due until paid at the lesser of (a) one and one-half percent (1.5%) per month and (b) the maximum rate permitted by applicable law.

**3.5** *Taxes.* Prairielands shall be responsible for any withholding taxes imposed by Argentina or Brazil on royalty payments. If Prairielands is required to withhold any tax, Prairielands shall (a) deduct the required amount, (b) remit the withheld amount to the appropriate taxing authority, and (c) provide Greenfield with official tax receipts or other evidence of payment.

**3.6** *No Pass-Through Obligation.* The Parties acknowledge that the Master License does not contain any provision requiring Greenfield to remit a portion of sublicensee royalties to NovaTrait, and accordingly, no royalty pass-through to NovaTrait is required under this Agreement as of the Effective Date.

**[Article 4 --- Records and Audit Rights]{.underline}**

**4.1** *Record Keeping.* Prairielands shall maintain complete and accurate books and records relating to the development, production, and sale of Sublicensed Products, including all records necessary to verify Net Sales calculations and royalty payments. Such records shall be maintained in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles consistently applied and shall be retained for a period of not less than five (5) years following the calendar year to which they relate.

**4.2** *Audit Rights.* Greenfield shall have the right, no more than **once per calendar year**, to engage an independent certified public accountant reasonably acceptable to Prairielands to examine and audit Prairielands\' books and records solely for the purpose of verifying the accuracy of royalty reports and payments under this Agreement. Greenfield shall provide Prairielands with at least **forty-five (45) days\' prior written notice** of any such audit. The audit shall be conducted during normal business hours at Prairielands\' offices and shall not unreasonably interfere with Prairielands\' operations.

**4.3** *Audit Results.* If an audit reveals an underpayment of royalties in excess of five percent (5%) for any calendar year, Prairielands shall (a) promptly pay the deficiency plus interest as provided in Section 3.4, and (b) reimburse Greenfield for the reasonable costs of the audit. If the audit reveals no underpayment or an underpayment of five percent (5%) or less, Greenfield shall bear the costs of the audit.

**4.4** *Confidentiality of Audit.* The independent accountant conducting the audit shall execute a confidentiality agreement reasonably satisfactory to Prairielands prior to commencing the audit. The accountant shall disclose to Greenfield only whether the royalty payments were accurate and, if not, the amount of any discrepancy, and shall not disclose any other financial or business information of Prairielands.

**[Article 5 --- Intellectual Property and Improvements]{.underline}**

**5.1** *Ownership of Licensed IP.* Prairielands acknowledges that NovaTrait is the owner of the Licensed IP and that nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to transfer or assign any ownership interest in the Licensed IP to Prairielands. Prairielands shall not challenge, contest, or take any action to impair or invalidate any patent, trade secret, or other intellectual property right included in the Licensed IP.

**5.2** *Improvements by Prairielands.* Any improvements, modifications, or enhancements to the Licensed Technology developed solely by Prairielands during the Term (\"**Prairielands Improvements**\") shall be owned by Prairielands. Prairielands hereby grants to Greenfield a non-exclusive, royalty-free license to use any Prairielands Improvements for internal research purposes only. For the avoidance of doubt, such license does not extend to NovaTrait unless separately agreed in writing.

**5.3** *Marking.* Prairielands shall include appropriate patent marking on all Sublicensed Products and associated packaging in compliance with the patent laws of Argentina and Brazil, as applicable, and shall mark products sold in any country where such marking is required to preserve patent rights.

**[Article 6 --- Confidentiality]{.underline}**

**6.1** *Obligations.* Each Party shall hold the other Party\'s Confidential Information in strict confidence, shall not disclose such Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the disclosing Party, and shall use such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of performing its obligations under this Agreement.

**6.2** *Exceptions.* The obligations of Section 6.1 shall not apply to information that: (a) is or becomes publicly available other than through a breach of this Agreement; (b) was known to the receiving Party prior to disclosure; (c) is independently developed by the receiving Party without use of or reference to the disclosing Party\'s Confidential Information; or (d) is required to be disclosed by law, regulation, or court order, provided that the receiving Party gives prompt notice to the disclosing Party and cooperates with any effort to obtain a protective order.

**6.3** *Duration.* The confidentiality obligations under this Article 6 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement for a period of five (5) years, except that obligations with respect to trade secrets shall continue for so long as the information remains a trade secret under applicable law.

**[Article 7 --- Term and Termination]{.underline}**

**7.1** *Term.* This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall remain in effect until **December 31, 2024**, unless earlier terminated in accordance with this Article 7. The Term of this Agreement is co-terminus with the Master License as in effect on the Effective Date.

**7.2** *Renewal.* This Agreement may be renewed only by mutual written agreement of the Parties, subject to the continued effectiveness of the Master License and NovaTrait\'s consent to the continued sublicense.

**7.3** *Termination for Cause.* Either Party may terminate this Agreement by written notice if the other Party:

> \(a\) commits a material breach of this Agreement and fails to cure such breach within sixty (60) days after receiving written notice specifying the breach in reasonable detail; or
>
> \(b\) becomes the subject of a voluntary or involuntary petition in bankruptcy, makes an assignment for the benefit of creditors, or is the subject of proceedings under any insolvency or receivership law.

**7.4** *Termination of Master License.* This Agreement shall automatically terminate upon the expiration or termination of the Master License for any reason, without further action by either Party.

**7.5** *Wind-Down Period.* Upon expiration or termination of this Agreement for any reason, Prairielands shall have a period of **ninety (90) days** (the \"**Wind-Down Period**\") during which Prairielands may:

> \(a\) sell off existing inventory of Sublicensed Products that was produced prior to the date of expiration or termination;
>
> \(b\) fulfill orders for Sublicensed Products that were placed by customers prior to the date of expiration or termination; and
>
> \(c\) complete any field trials or regulatory submissions in progress as of the date of expiration or termination, provided that no new plantings may be initiated during the Wind-Down Period.

Prairielands shall continue to report Net Sales and pay royalties in accordance with Article 3 for all Sublicensed Products sold during the Wind-Down Period.

**7.6** *Return of Materials.* Within thirty (30) days following the expiration of the Wind-Down Period, Prairielands shall return to Greenfield (or, at Greenfield\'s election, destroy with written certification of destruction) all proprietary expression vectors, biological materials, technical documentation, and other materials embodying or relating to the Licensed Technology in Prairielands\' possession or control.

**7.7** *Surviving Provisions.* Articles 4 (Records and Audit Rights), 5 (Intellectual Property), 6 (Confidentiality), 8 (Indemnification and Limitation of Liability), and 9 (General Provisions) shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement.

**[Article 8 --- Indemnification and Limitation of Liability]{.underline}**

**8.1** *Indemnification by Prairielands.* Prairielands shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Greenfield and its directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or relating to (a) Prairielands\' use of the Licensed Technology outside the scope of the sublicense granted herein, (b) any Sublicensed Product sold by Prairielands, including product liability claims, or (c) Prairielands\' breach of this Agreement.

**8.2** *Indemnification by Greenfield.* Greenfield shall indemnify, defend, and hold harmless Prairielands and its directors, officers, employees, and agents from and against any and all claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses (including reasonable attorneys\' fees) arising out of or relating to any claim that the Licensed Technology, as used by Prairielands in compliance with this Agreement, infringes any third-party intellectual property right, provided that Greenfield\'s indemnity obligation under this Section 8.2 is subject to and limited by the scope of any corresponding indemnity Greenfield receives from NovaTrait under the Master License.

**8.3** *Limitation of Liability.* EXCEPT FOR BREACHES OF ARTICLE 6 (CONFIDENTIALITY) OR OBLIGATIONS UNDER SECTION 8.1 OR 8.2 (INDEMNIFICATION), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE TO THE OTHER FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF OR RELATING TO THIS AGREEMENT, REGARDLESS OF THE THEORY OF LIABILITY, EVEN IF SUCH PARTY HAS BEEN ADVISED OF THE POSSIBILITY OF SUCH DAMAGES.

**8.4** *Cap on Liability.* Except for Prairielands\' obligation to pay royalties and for claims arising under Section 8.1 or 8.2, each Party\'s aggregate liability under this Agreement shall not exceed the total royalty payments made or payable by Prairielands in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim.

**[Article 9 --- General Provisions]{.underline}**

**9.1** *Notices.* All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be deemed delivered when (a) delivered personally, (b) sent by nationally recognized overnight courier service, or (c) sent by certified mail, return receipt requested, to the addresses set forth in the preamble of this Agreement, or to such other address as a Party may designate in writing.

**9.2** *Governing Law.* This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without regard to its conflict of laws principles.

**9.3** *Dispute Resolution.* Any dispute arising out of or relating to this Agreement shall be resolved first through good-faith negotiation between senior executives of the Parties. If the dispute is not resolved within thirty (30) days, either Party may submit the dispute to binding arbitration in Raleigh, North Carolina, administered by the American Arbitration Association under its Commercial Arbitration Rules.

**9.4** *Entire Agreement.* This Agreement, including Exhibit A attached hereto, constitutes the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior and contemporaneous agreements, understandings, negotiations, and discussions, whether oral or written, between the Parties relating to such subject matter.

**9.5** *Amendment.* This Agreement may not be amended or modified except by a written instrument executed by both Parties.

**9.6** *Waiver.* No waiver of any provision of this Agreement shall be effective unless made in writing and signed by the waiving Party. No waiver of any breach or default shall constitute a waiver of any subsequent breach or default.

**9.7** *Severability.* If any provision of this Agreement is held to be invalid, illegal, or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

**9.8** *Assignment.* Neither Party may assign this Agreement or any rights hereunder without the prior written consent of the other Party, except that Greenfield may assign this Agreement to a successor in connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of all or substantially all of its assets, subject to the terms of the Master License.

**9.9** *Relationship of the Parties.* The Parties are independent contractors. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create a partnership, joint venture, agency, or employment relationship between the Parties.

**9.10** *Counterparts.* This Agreement may be executed in counterparts, each of which shall be deemed an original, and all of which together shall constitute one and the same instrument.

**IN WITNESS WHEREOF**, the Parties have executed this Sublicense Agreement as of the date first written above.

result:
**CLARENDON INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY CONSULTING, LLC**

**EXECUTIVE SUMMARY**

**Patent Landscape Analysis: NovaTrait Genomics Corporation** **Licensed Technology Portfolios --- Gene-Editing, Trait Expression & Bioinformatics Platforms**

Prepared for Greenfield Biosciences, Inc.

**Date of Report:** September 14, 2020 **Engagement Reference:** CPC-2020-0447 **Classification:** CONFIDENTIAL --- ATTORNEY-CLIENT PRIVILEGED

**Prepared by:**

> Dr. Nadia Petrov, Ph.D., J.D. Lead Consultant, Patent Valuation & Landscape Analysis Clarendon Intellectual Property Consulting, LLC
>
> **Commissioned by:**
>
> Greenfield Biosciences, Inc. 4200 Research Park Drive, Suite 310 Raleigh, NC 27609
>
> Attn: Terence Blackwood, General Counsel

**[1. Scope and Purpose]{.underline}**

This Executive Summary presents the principal findings and recommendations arising from Clarendon Intellectual Property Consulting, LLC\'s comprehensive patent landscape analysis of the intellectual property portfolios underlying three technology licenses granted by NovaTrait Genomics Corporation (\"NovaTrait\") to Greenfield Biosciences, Inc. (\"Greenfield\"). The analysis was conducted at the request of Greenfield\'s General Counsel, Terence Blackwood, in coordination with outside counsel Victoria Chen at Ashworth & Calder LLP, to inform strategic planning with respect to the long-term value and defensibility of the licensed intellectual property.

The scope of this engagement encompassed the following workstreams:

> • Full prior art and prosecution history analysis for the patent families underlying **License A** (CRISPR-Ag Suite gene-editing platform) and **License B** (TraitForge plant trait expression platform);
>
> • Competitive landscape mapping of third-party patent filings in the agricultural gene-editing and trait expression technology domains;
>
> • Freedom-to-operate assessment for Greenfield\'s current and near-term product pipeline;
>
> • Valuation weighting of individual patents within each licensed portfolio; and
>
> • Forward-looking risk assessment and strategic recommendations regarding license renewal positioning.

The full report (447 pages including claim charts, prosecution timeline maps, and patent family trees) has been transmitted separately under cover letter dated September 14, 2020. This Executive Summary distills the key findings relevant to Greenfield\'s commercial decision-making and forthcoming license renewal discussions.

**[2. License A --- CRISPR-Ag Suite Gene-Editing Platform Portfolio]{.underline}**

**2.1 Portfolio Composition**

The License A portfolio consists of four U.S. patents and their corresponding foreign counterparts (EP, BR, AR, and AU):

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Patent No.**   **Description**                                                                    **Issue Date**   **Expiry Date**   **Value Weighting**
  ---------------- ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------- ----------------- ---------------------
  9,234,117        Foundational CRISPR-based gene-editing method for monocot and dicot crop species   2016             March 12, 2028    \~60%

  9,234,118        Guide RNA design and delivery optimization for plant cell transformation           2016             2033 (est.)       \~15%

  10,112,445       Improved editing efficiency and off-target reduction in Zea mays applications      2018             October 6, 2035   \~18%

  9,651,002        Selectable marker systems for identifying successfully edited plant cells          2017             2034 (est.)       \~7%
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**2.2 Overall Portfolio Strength Rating**

**PORTFOLIO STRENGTH: STRONG**

The CRISPR-Ag Suite portfolio is anchored by U.S. Patent No. 9,234,117, which we assess as the single most commercially significant patent in the agricultural gene-editing space as of the date of this analysis. This foundational patent claims broad methods for CRISPR-based editing of both monocot and dicot plant genomes, with specific embodiments directed to herbicide tolerance and insect resistance trait insertion in corn (*Zea mays*) and soybean (*Glycine max*). The patent\'s prosecution history reflects a robust examination record: all 23 original claims survived prosecution without substantive amendment, and no inter partes review proceedings have been initiated against it to date.

**Critical finding: U.S. Patent No. 9,234,117 represents approximately 60% of the assessed commercial value of the entire License A portfolio.** This weighting reflects its broad independent claim scope, its status as the foundational method patent upon which the improvement patents depend, and the absence of viable design-around alternatives for its core claimed methods in the near term. The remaining three patents provide meaningful but incremental value --- they cover optimization techniques, delivery systems, and selection methods that enhance the core editing technology but do not independently confer the ability to perform gene editing in agricultural applications.

The improvement patent (U.S. Patent No. 10,112,445), while valuable for its efficiency gains in *Zea mays* applications, has narrower claim scope and is dependent on the methods claimed in the \'117 patent. Its independent commercial utility post-expiry of the \'117 patent will be significantly diminished, though it does provide some protection for specific Greenfield product lines through October 2035.

**2.3 Patent Expiry and Declining Exclusivity Analysis**

The expiry of U.S. Patent No. 9,234,117 on **March 12, 2028** is the single most important temporal event in this landscape analysis. Upon expiry:

> • The core CRISPR-based gene-editing methods claimed therein will enter the public domain, permitting any third party to practice those methods without a license from NovaTrait.
>
> • The remaining patents in the portfolio (Nos. 9,234,118; 9,651,002; and 10,112,445) will retain their enforceability for their respective remaining terms, but their collective value absent the foundational patent is substantially reduced --- we estimate the residual portfolio value at approximately **35--40% of the current aggregate portfolio value** once the \'117 patent expires.
>
> • Competitors that have already developed improvement patents (see Section 2.4 below) will be free to combine their proprietary improvements with the now-public-domain foundational methods, creating commercially viable alternative platforms without requiring a NovaTrait license.

**Strategic implication for renewal:** If Greenfield is presented with a renewal proposal for License A extending beyond March 2028, the royalty structure for the post-expiry period (approximately the final two years of a five-year renewal beginning mid-2025) should reflect the significantly diminished patent exclusivity. A uniform royalty rate applied across the entire renewal term would result in Greenfield overpaying for the final two years relative to the actual intellectual property value received. We recommend that any renewal incorporate a step-down royalty mechanism or that the parties agree to a rate renegotiation trigger upon expiry of the \'117 patent.

**2.4 Competitive Landscape --- Third-Party Patent Filings**

Our analysis identified **47 patent families** filed by third parties in the agricultural CRISPR gene-editing space between 2016 and mid-2020. Of these, two competitors warrant particular attention:

**AgriGenome Solutions** has filed a series of seven improvement patents directed to next-generation guide RNA architectures and modified Cas protein variants optimized for cereal crop applications. Three of these patents have issued (as of the date of this report), and the remaining four are in prosecution. These patents do not directly challenge NovaTrait\'s foundational claims during the life of the \'117 patent, but they position AgriGenome Solutions to offer a competitive gene-editing platform once the \'117 patent expires in 2028. AgriGenome Solutions\' improvement patents are expected to remain in force through at least 2037, giving them a significant window of competitive advantage in the post-2028 landscape.

**CropTech Innovations** has pursued a distinct approach, filing four patent applications directed to base-editing (as opposed to double-strand break CRISPR editing) methods in plant systems. While base-editing technologies represent a partially overlapping but technically distinct field, CropTech Innovations\' filings include claims that could encompass certain applications currently within NovaTrait\'s licensed field of use. CropTech Innovations\' patents, if and when granted, may provide Greenfield and other licensees with alternative technological pathways that do not require a NovaTrait license for certain trait insertion applications.

**Combined competitive impact:** The filings by AgriGenome Solutions and CropTech Innovations collectively indicate that NovaTrait\'s current near-monopoly position in agricultural gene-editing platforms will erode significantly after 2028. By 2029--2030, we project that at least two commercially viable alternative platforms will be available, which will exert substantial downward pressure on market royalty rates for gene-editing technology licenses. Current market royalty rates for gene-editing platform licenses in the agricultural biotech sector range from **3.5% to 5.5% of Net Sales**, with the higher end of that range reflecting NovaTrait\'s current dominant position. Post-2028, we would expect equilibrium rates to gravitate toward the lower end of that range or below, as the competitive landscape expands and the foundational methods enter the public domain.

**[3. License B --- TraitForge Plant Trait Expression Platform Portfolio]{.underline}**

**3.1 Portfolio Composition**

The License B portfolio consists of three U.S. patents and their foreign counterparts (EP, BR):

  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **Patent No.**   **Description**                                                               **Issue Date**   **Expiry Date**   **Value Weighting**
  ---------------- ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- ---------------- ----------------- ---------------------
  10,443,076       Proprietary promoter-enhancer system for drought tolerance trait expression   2019             2036 (est.)       \~40%

  10,443,077       Multi-gene expression cassette architecture for stacked trait delivery        2019             2036 (est.)       \~35%

  10,889,201       Nutrient uptake efficiency expression system in Glycine max                   2021 (est.)      2038 (est.)       \~25%
  -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**3.2 Overall Portfolio Strength Rating**

**PORTFOLIO STRENGTH: VERY STRONG**

The TraitForge portfolio presents a materially different risk profile than the CRISPR-Ag Suite portfolio. All three patents were issued relatively recently, with expiry dates extending through at least 2036 and potentially 2038. The claims are well-drafted, with robust specification support and a prosecution history free of significant estoppel concerns.

More critically, our landscape search identified **no close commercial alternatives** to the TraitForge platform as of the date of this analysis. The proprietary promoter-enhancer system claimed in U.S. Patent No. 10,443,076 represents a genuinely novel approach to drought tolerance trait expression that has no equivalent in either the published patent literature or the commercial marketplace. The multi-gene expression cassette architecture (No. 10,443,077) enables trait stacking capabilities that competing platforms cannot replicate without infringing NovaTrait\'s claims.

**Key finding:** NovaTrait\'s TraitForge portfolio confers a degree of market exclusivity that is qualitatively different from, and significantly stronger than, the CRISPR-Ag Suite portfolio. There are no viable design-around strategies available to competitors in the near or medium term, and the patent terms extend well beyond any plausible renewal period. This irreplaceability must be weighed carefully in any renewal negotiation.

**3.3 Competitive Landscape --- Absence of Alternatives**

We conducted exhaustive searches of the U.S., European, Brazilian, and international (PCT) patent databases, as well as published scientific literature and commercial product databases. The results confirm:

> • No third-party patent filings were identified that claim methods or compositions substantially similar to the TraitForge expression system.
>
> • Internal development of a comparable platform by Greenfield or any competitor would require significant de novo research --- we estimate a timeline of **3 to 4 years** and substantial capital investment to achieve functional equivalence, with no guarantee of success.
>
> • The biological materials (proprietary expression vectors) associated with the TraitForge license are not independently available from any other source, creating an additional layer of dependency beyond the patent rights themselves.

This analysis supports the conclusion that NovaTrait possesses significant negotiating leverage with respect to License B, and that Greenfield\'s strategic position is best served by securing long-term renewal terms at commercially reasonable rates rather than attempting to develop or source alternatives.

**[4. License C --- GenoMap Pro Bioinformatics Platform]{.underline}**

While License C (GenoMap Pro) was not the primary focus of this patent landscape analysis, we note for completeness that the GenoMap Pro platform is protected principally by copyright (U.S. Copyright Registration No. TX-8-912-445) and trade secrets rather than patents. The bioinformatics software market is more competitive than the gene-editing or trait expression domains, with at least three commercially available alternatives identified: BioSphere Analytics, PlantLogic Suite, and TraitView Pro. Annual fees for comparable platforms range from approximately **\$2.5 million to \$4.0 million**. The existence of viable alternatives provides Greenfield with meaningful negotiating leverage on License C that is not available with respect to Licenses A and B.

**[5. Summary of Risk Ratings]{.underline}**

  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
  **License**                   **Portfolio Strength**              **Competitive Alternatives**   **Renewal Risk to Greenfield**     **Licensor Leverage**
  ----------------------------- ----------------------------------- ------------------------------ ---------------------------------- ------------------------
  License A (CRISPR-Ag Suite)   Strong                              Emerging (post-2028)           Moderate --- declining over term   Moderate --- declining

  License B (TraitForge)        Very Strong                         None identified                High --- irreplaceable             Very High

  License C (GenoMap Pro)       Moderate (copyright/trade secret)   Multiple alternatives exist    Low --- substitutable              Low to Moderate
  ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**[6. Strategic Recommendations]{.underline}**

Based on the foregoing analysis, Clarendon Intellectual Property Consulting, LLC offers the following strategic recommendations to Greenfield:

1\. **License A renewal terms should incorporate a royalty step-down mechanism** tied to the expiry of U.S. Patent No. 9,234,117 on March 12, 2028. A renewal extending to mid-2030 would include approximately two years during which the foundational patent --- representing 60% of the portfolio\'s value --- is no longer in force. Greenfield should not agree to a flat royalty rate across the entire renewal term that does not account for this declining value. Any proposed rate that exceeds the upper boundary of current market rates (3.5%--5.5%) before accounting for the \'117 patent expiry should be resisted, and rates for the post-2028 period should be substantially lower --- potentially in the range of 2.0%--3.5% --- reflecting the residual value of the remaining improvement patents and know-how.

2\. **Any proposed expansion of the License A field of use** (e.g., to include additional crop species) must be evaluated against the actual scope of the underlying patents. Our analysis of the claims of the four licensed patents indicates that certain proposed field-of-use expansions may extend into areas not fully covered by the existing patent claims. Greenfield should not accept an expanded field-of-use accompanied by a higher royalty rate unless NovaTrait can demonstrate that its patent portfolio provides meaningful exclusivity in the expanded field. This is particularly relevant where NovaTrait\'s patents may not include specific embodiments or data for newly added crop species --- the value of the license is diminished if the underlying patents do not actually block third-party competition in the expanded field.

3\. **License B should be treated as a critical strategic asset** in Greenfield\'s negotiations. The absence of commercial alternatives and the long patent terms give NovaTrait substantial leverage. Greenfield should seek the longest possible renewal term for License B to lock in rates before NovaTrait can seek further increases. At the same time, Greenfield should be vigilant regarding any proposed royalty rate that exceeds the upper end of market rates for trait expression platform licenses (currently 3.0%--4.5% of Net Sales). A proposed rate above this range should be challenged as exceeding fair market value, even accounting for the platform\'s irreplaceability.

4\. **License C presents the strongest negotiating position for Greenfield.** The availability of multiple competing bioinformatics platforms at comparable or lower price points provides credible leverage. Any proposed fee increase above the market range of \$2.5 million to \$4.0 million annually should be resisted, and Greenfield should obtain competitive proposals from alternative providers to support its negotiating position.

5\. **An updated patent landscape analysis should be commissioned** prior to the commencement of any formal renewal negotiations, given that the competitive landscape in agricultural gene-editing is evolving rapidly. This recommendation is particularly urgent with respect to the AgriGenome Solutions and CropTech Innovations patent portfolios, which were still in early prosecution stages at the time of this analysis and may have materially advanced.

**Disclaimer:** This Executive Summary is provided for the confidential use of Greenfield Biosciences, Inc. and its legal advisors. It does not constitute legal advice. Clarendon Intellectual Property Consulting, LLC is an independent consulting firm and has no financial interest in the outcome of any licensing negotiation between Greenfield and NovaTrait. The opinions expressed herein are based on information available as of September 14, 2020, and may require revision in light of subsequent developments in patent prosecution, litigation, or market conditions.

CLARENDON INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY CONSULTING, LLC

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")

"""Generate a .docx from markdown via Pandoc with an optional reference template.

Usage:
    python generate_from_md.py input.md output.docx [template.docx]

Pandoc applies the template's paragraph and run styles to your markdown.
Best for reports/memos/letters where the template carries firm styling.

Requires pandoc on PATH.
"""
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path


def generate(md_path: Path, output_path: Path, template_path: Path | None = None):
    output_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    cmd = ["pandoc", str(md_path), "-o", str(output_path)]
    if template_path is not None:
        cmd.append(f"--reference-doc={template_path}")
    result = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True)
    if result.returncode != 0:
        print(f"pandoc failed: {result.stderr}", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(1)
    print(f"OK: wrote {output_path}")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    if len(sys.argv) not in (3, 4):
        print("Usage: generate_from_md.py <input.md> <output.docx> [template.docx]", file=sys.stderr)
        sys.exit(2)
    template = Path(sys.argv[3]) if len(sys.argv) == 4 else None
    generate(Path(sys.argv[1]), Path(sys.argv[2]), template)
